name: Apptio Technology Business Management API
what_it_does: Technology Business Management (TBM) and FinOps platform with REST API — providing IT financial management, cloud cost allocation, technology spend modeling, cost-to-value analysis, and benchmarking for enterprise technology organizations to connect technology spending to business outcomes.
use_cases: Querying cloud cost allocation data by business unit, product, or cost center via API
Pulling technology spending reports for executive financial dashboards
Syncing cloud billing data from AWS, Azure, and GCP into Apptio for allocation
Integrating Apptio showback data with internal chargeback systems
Automating technology spend reporting for quarterly business reviews
not_for: Lightweight cloud cost monitoring (use CloudHealth, Vantage, or CAST AI for simpler needs)
Real-time cloud resource optimization (use cloud-native cost tools first)
SMB companies without dedicated IT finance function
best_when: A large enterprise with $10M+ IT spend needs to model, allocate, and communicate technology costs to business stakeholders using TBM methodology, with cloud and on-premises data.
avoid_when: You need simple cloud cost visibility, are a startup or mid-market, or don't have dedicated IT finance and TBM program.

auth.notes: OAuth2 client credentials flow via IBM Cloud identity (Apptio acquired by IBM in 2019). Client ID and secret from Apptio/IBM account. Access token for API calls. No webhooks. Enterprise deployment — API access requires IBM/Apptio enterprise agreement.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing via IBM; contact IBM sales
pricing.notes: Seattle-based company. Founded 2007. Acquired by IBM in 2019 for $2B. TBM Council co-founder. FinOps Foundation member. 500+ enterprise customers including Fortune 500. Cloudability product for cloud FinOps. Apptio Targetprocess for product portfolio management. Strong in large enterprises with complex IT cost allocation needs.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for cost models, allocations, reports, and data ingestion. OAuth2 via IBM Cloud IAM. Limited public API documentation — enterprise partner documentation. TBM data model understanding required for meaningful queries. IBM acquisition created API documentation fragmentation between old Apptio and IBM identity systems.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Cost report reads idempotent by time period. Allocation reads idempotent. Data ingestion idempotent by identifier. Model reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: IBM acquisition — authentication now uses IBM Cloud IAM, not legacy Apptio auth
TBM data model must be understood — cost pools, towers, and allocations are complex
API documentation fragmented between Apptio legacy docs and IBM integration docs
Enterprise agreement required for API access — not self-service
Data ingestion from cloud providers is separate from API data querying

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP compliant. GDPR compliant. US and EU data residency. IBM Cloud IAM OAuth2. Financial spend data is highly sensitive — enterprise security controls. IBM acquisition brings enterprise-grade security infrastructure.
